food_name: Rose Apple (Jambu Air / Water Apple, fresh, Syzygium aqueum)

local_names.romanized_local: Lián Wù (Chinese); Jambu Air (Malay/Indonesian); Neer Naval (Tamil); Jal Seb (Hindi); Chompoo (Thai)

region_of_origin: Southeast Asia (native); widely cultivated in Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia, Thailand
food_subgroup: Fresh tropical fruit with edible thin skin; very high water content (~90–93%); extremely low caloric density; mild, barely sweet flavour

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a dedicated entry for Syzygium aqueum (jambu air / water apple). FOODON_03301710 is the closest parent node for rose apple / tropical Syzygium fruit. Exact species-level entry is absent.
ontology.foodex2_description: Rose apple (Syzygium aqueum) — mapped to FoodEx2 group A00LC (Tropical and subtropical fruits, edible peel) as closest defensible parent. No species-specific FoodEx2 code exists.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No species-specific FoodEx2 code for Syzygium aqueum. A00LC (tropical fruits with edible peel) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept identified for rose apple / jambu air / Syzygium aqueum as of April 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of rose apple (jambu air) is 2–3 medium fruits weighing approximately 150g, commonly eaten fresh as a snack or alongside meals in Singapore and Southeast Asia.

nutrient_source.title: Rose Apple / Jambu Air — Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo) and FAO/INFOODS Food Composition Table for Southeast Asia
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) and FAO/INFOODS SEA
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition table for Syzygium aqueum (jambu air). Both are approved primary sources for Southeast Asian foods. Values are consistent across both sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Rose Apple / Syzygium aqueum — University of Sydney GI Database and FAO/INFOODS SEA contextual reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~22 (very low) referenced from University of Sydney GI Database for Syzygium species and FAO/INFOODS SEA contextual values. Glycemic load per 150g serving = (22 × 5.7 × 1.5) / 100 = 1.88, rounded to 1.9. Confidence 0.9 — direct GI measurement for Syzygium aqueum is limited; value is consistent with published Syzygium samarangense GI data and the fruit's very low sugar and very high water content.

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium (0 mg/100g); moderate potassium (123 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Rose apple is an excellent choice for hypertensive patients: it contains essentially zero sodium and provides potassium (123 mg/100g), supporting healthy blood pressure regulation; unrestricted consumption is appropriate.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low GI (~22) and very low glycemic load per serving (GL 1.9 per 150g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Rose apple (jambu air) is one of the safest fruits for Type 2 Diabetes patients: with a GI of ~22 and a GL of only 1.9 per 150g serving, it can be consumed freely without meaningful glycemic impact, and clinical dietitians should actively recommend it as a preferred daily snack.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, zero trans fat; low caloric density (25 kcal/100g); vitamin C (22.2 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Rose apple is cardioprotective: it contains no saturated fat, no cholesterol, and no trans fat, while providing vitamin C (22.2 mg/100g) which supports vascular health; its very low energy density also supports healthy weight management in CVD patients.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Vitamin C (22.2 mg/100g); high water content supports joint hydration
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Rose apple provides vitamin C (22.2 mg/100g) which supports collagen synthesis and antioxidant defence relevant to RA; its very high water content (~90%) contributes to systemic hydration, which benefits joint lubrication.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rose Apple (Jambu Air / Water Apple; Syzygium aqueum) is a bell-shaped tropical fruit native to Southeast Asia, widely grown in Singapore home gardens and sold at wet markets and hawker fruit stalls. The fruit has a thin, waxy, edible skin ranging from white to deep pink-red, with snow-white or pale crisp flesh and an extraordinary water content of approximately 90–93%, making each bite explosively juicy and refreshing. The flavour is extremely mild — barely sweet, faintly floral — more akin to fragrant, crispy water than conventional fruit sweetness. At only 25 kcal per 100g, jambu air has one of the lowest caloric densities of any fruit. Its glycemic index is approximately 22 (very low), yielding a glycemic load of just 1.9 per standard 150g serving (2–3 fruits). This makes it one of the most glycemically safe fruits available and a genuinely unrestricted food for Type 2 Diabetes patients — a rare and clinically significant distinction. Core macros per 100g: energy 25 kcal, protein 0.6g, fat 0.3g, carbohydrate 5.7g, sugar 5.0g, dietary fibre 0.5g, sodium 0 mg. Micronutrients include vitamin C (22.2 mg), potassium (123 mg), calcium (29 mg), and niacin (0.8 mg). The fruit is vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. In Singapore, jambu air is culturally familiar, affordable, and widely available — making it one of the most practical dietary recommendations for T2DM patients, hypertensive patients, and those managing cardiovascular risk.
common_user_queries: is jambu air good for diabetes

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (primary approved source for Singapore foods) and cross-referenced with FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition table. Both are approved primary sources. GI value (~22) is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database references for Syzygium species. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for fresh rose apple (Syzygium aqueum) is not available in HPB Singapore Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA table as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: precise moisture content not separately reported in the primary source tables used (approximate water content ~90–93% is widely cited in literature but not as a tabulated per_100g value in the approved sources). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant food, naturally contains no vitamin B12). folate_mcg_dfe is 0.0 (confirmed zero per HPB/FAO source; rose apple is not a significant folate source). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant food, no significant vitamin D). saturated_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed negligible per approved sources). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (naturally zero — plant food). sodium_mg is 0.0 (confirmed negligible per approved sources). GI value of ~22 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database references for Syzygium species; direct GI measurement for Syzygium aqueum is limited in published literature. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 used as closest parent — no species-specific FoodOn entry exists for Syzygium aqueum. FoodEx2 code A00LC used as closest parent — no species-specific FoodEx2 code exists.
